design_philosophy.core_loop: Plan mission loadout → Fly stealth approach → Execute objectives while maintaining radar signature discipline → Extract and debrief
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk of detection vs. mission effectiveness. Staying undetected maintains diplomatic safety; being detected escalates to combat or diplomatic incident depending on difficulty mode.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an advanced stealth fighter on covert military operations across real-world hotspots, relying on stealth tactics and technical knowledge rather than firepower
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game treats stealth as a learnable skill requiring player education. It includes comprehensive documentation on aerodynamics, radar theory, and stealth technology. Difficulty modes (conventional, limited, cold war) reflect real geopolitical constraints. The game rewards preparation and knowledge over reflexes.

difficulty.lives_system: Permanent pilot fatalities with persistent campaign consequences
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpoint structure. Player is rescued on successful extraction or captured/killed on failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High player agency: can select mission difficulty, choose geographic theater (Libya, Persian Gulf, North Cape, Central Europe), select ordnance loadout, and choose difficulty mode (conventional/limited/cold war). Cold war mode adds diplomatic incident penalty for detection.

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment selection system allowing choice of realistic ordnance and aircraft configuration before each mission
progression.unlock_mechanism: Pilot roster system tracks rank, score, and medals across campaign. Pilot advancement tied to mission success and survival
replayability_hooks: Permanent pilot fatalities create campaign consequences
Multiple geographic theaters with different tactical requirements
Three difficulty modes with different consequences
AI units behave dynamically based on player detection status

world_building_techniques: Four distinct geographic warzone settings (Gaddafi's Libya, Persian Gulf, North Cape, Central Europe) with real geopolitical context
Mission briefing system with situational context
Dynamic mission outcomes reflecting real-world consequences (pilot rescue, capture headlines, diplomatic protests)
Pilot roster with name, rank, medals creating persistent character narrative
AI behavior reflecting military doctrine (patrol → aggressive search on detection)

faction_indicators: Cold War superpowers (U.S. vs. USSR technology representation)
NATO vs. Soviet bloc (North Cape, Central Europe theaters)
danger_signals: Enemy aircraft patrol aggression on detection
Radar station search patterns escalating with alert level
Diplomatic incident in cold war mode upon detection
environmental_storytelling: Real-world geographic locations with historically accurate military equipment
Realistic radar behavior and stealth signature mechanics reflect actual military technology
Mission outcomes (Pravda headlines, rescue operations, diplomatic protests) reflect geopolitical context
